titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Willis Photographs of Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota
Willis (John) Photographs of Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota
titlestmt.author: Sarah Lerner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 15975291.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 20, 2021 at 03:58

did.unittitle: Photographs of Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites and persons at Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and in South Dakota, 2015-2016. Identified locations include Batesland, Black Hills, Kyle, Lost Dog Creek, Manderson, Mount Rushmore, Oglala, Porcupine, Potato Creek, Red Shirt Table, Slim Buttes, Wanblee, and Wounded Knee.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Willis on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Photographs from Unidentified Locations within Pine Ridge Reservation and South Dakota, 2015-2016. II. Photographs from Identified Locations within Pine Ridge Reservation and South Dakota, 2015-2016.

bioghist.head: John Willis (born 1957)
bioghist.p: John Willis is a documentary photographer and emeritus professor of photography at Marlboro College in Marlboro, Vermont. His monographs include Recycled Realities , co-authored with Tom Young (Center for American Places, 2002), Views from the Reservation (Center for American Places, 2010), and Mni Wiconi - Water Is Life Honoring the Water Protectors at Standing Rock and Everywhere in the Ongoing Struggle for Indigenous Sovereignty (Staunton, Virginia: George F. Thompson Publishing, 2019). Willis has photographs in the permanent collections of the Whitney Museum of American Art, the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston, and the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston, among others. He is also a co-founder with Bill Ledger of the In-Sight Photography Project, a photography outreach project to youth in Brattleboro, Vermont.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Willis, Photographs of Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Many discrete images share the same title throughout the collection.
Titled, signed, and dated by the photographer on versos of prints.
Titles in this guide are from the inscriptions by the photographer on versos of prints.
Each folder in the collection contains a single inkjet print.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet prints of photographs by John Willis that document sites and persons at Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and in South Dakota, 2015-2016. Identified locations include Batesland, Black Hills, Kyle, Lost Dog Creek, Manderson, Mount Rushmore, Oglala, Porcupine, Potato Creek, Red Shirt Table, Slim Buttes, Wanblee, and Wounded Knee.
Individual prints are 13 x 19 inches (32.8 x 48.2 centimeters) and 24 x 31 inches (61 x 78.7 centimeters). Titled, signed, and dated by the photographer on versos of prints.

did.unittitle: Photographs from Unidentified Locations within Pine Ridge Reservation and South Dakota

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is arranged chronologically and then alphabetically by caption title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series I contains photographs taken by John Willis of unidentified locations within Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota.

title.part: Cooling off in the Horse Bucket
Davis Yellow Bull Cooling off in the Horses Watering Trough
Delores Yellow Bull a Few Months before She Passed Over
Kevin Poor Bear's Wife Making a Dancing Shawl
Yellow Bull Family Kitchen, a Passed on Katrina Trailer from New Orleans
Santana Little Spotted Horse Miss Lakota Nation
Santana Little Spotted Horse Miss Lakota Nation

did.unittitle: Photographs from Identified Locations within Pine Ridge Reservation and South Dakota

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is arranged alphabetically by identified locations within Pine Ridge Indian Reservation and South Dakota.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series II contains photographs taken by John Willis of Batesland, Black Hills, Kyle, Lost Dog Creek, Manderson, Mount Rushmore, Oglala, Porcupine, Potato Creek, Red Shirt Table, Slim Buttes, Wanblee, and Wounded Knee.

title.part: Mercedes Little Spirited Horse 4th of July Pow Wow
Mountain Pine Beetle Damage, Harney Peak, Hinhan Kaga as Named by Lakota People
Genevieve Holding Pow Wow Photo of Herself
Genevieve on Her Bed in Trailer Home Telling of Financial Difficulties
Little Wound High School Science Class
Little Wound School Elementary Hall Way
Heading Out to Feed the Horses, March Snow
March Morning, Singing Horse Trading Post
Singing Horse Trading Post, Number 1 of 2
Singing Horse Trading Post, Number 2 of 2
Singing Horse Trading Post, Rosie's Dog "Buffalo," Number 1 of 2
Singing Horse Trading Post, March Storm, Number 2 of 2
Baptist Church "Bible Thumper" Impromptu Prayer Service and Concert, Oglala Housing
Andrea Marshall with Great Grandson Kayden, Number 1 of 3
Andrea Marshall with Great Grandson Kayden, Number 2 of 3
Andrea Marshall with Great Grandson Kayden, Number 3 of 3
Charlyn and Adelita with Their Newly Adopted Son Kayeden
Charlyn Bull Bear and Adelita with Newly Adopted Son Kayeden
Angel Spider and Michael Two Bulls
Angel Spider and Michael Two Bulls
Angel Spider and Michael Two Bulls
Angel Spider and Michael Two Bulls
Eagle Medicine Sundance Grounds Welcome Sign
Monument for Those Who Served, Crazy Horse School

did.unittitle: [Tree in a field at Wounded Knee]
